questions.text: The lock and key model of enzyme action was proposed by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Louis Pasteur (1822–1895) was a French chemist and microbiologist who made pioneering contributions to the fields of microbiology and immunology. Louis Pasteur developed the process of pasteurization, which involves heating liquids (such as milk or wine) to a specific temperature to kill or deactivate bacteria and other microorganisms.

Emil Fischer (1890) proposed a Lock and Key model to visualize substrate and enzyme interaction. According to this model, as one specific key can open only a specific lock, in the same manner a specific enzyme can transform only one substrate into products(s). According to Lock and Key Model the active site is a rigid structure. There is no modification or fexibility in the active site before, during or after the enzyme action and it is used only as a template. Later studies did not support this model in all reactions.

Daniel E. Koshland Jr. (1920–2007) was a renowned American biochemist known for his significant contributions to the field of enzymology and molecular biology. Koshland is perhaps best known for proposing the "induced fit" model of enzyme-substrate binding. In this model, the active site of an enzyme undergoes a conformational change upon binding with the substrate, ensuring a more precise fit and enhancing catalytic activity.

The well-known scientific experiment related to the origin of life conducted by Stanley Miller and Harold Urey is often referred to as the "Miller-Urey experiment." Stanley L. Miller and Harold C. Urey conducted this groundbreaking experiment in 1952 to simulate the conditions thought to be present on early Earth. The goal was to investigate whether organic compounds, such as amino acids, the building blocks of proteins, could be formed under these conditions. The experiment involved creating an atmosphere containing gases believed to be present on early Earth (such as methane, ammonia, and water vapor) and subjecting it to simulated lightning (electrical discharge). The result was the production of various organic molecules, including amino acids.

questions.text: Which cell is the longest in the human body?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Muscle cells, also known as myocytes, are not typically considered the longest cells in the human body. While they can be relatively long, they are not as long as neurons. Muscle cells are cylindrical and can range in size from a few micrometers to several micrometrescentimetres in length, depending on the type of muscle they make up.

The longest cell in the human body is the neuron or nerve cell. Some neurons can be up to three or four feet long, stretching from the base of the spine down to the toes. These long neurons are called the "longest axons" and are found in the human body's peripheral nervous system.

Bone cells, also known as osteocytes, are not typically considered the longest cells in the human body either. They are embedded within the mineralized matrix of bone tissue and are relatively small in size, ranging from about 7-25 micrometers in diameter.

Gland cells are a type of specialized cell that is responsible for producing and secreting various types of substances such as hormones, enzymes, mucus, sweat, and saliva. There are many different types of gland cells in the human body, such as the cells that make up the thyroid gland, the pancreas, and the salivary glands. While gland cells can vary in size and shape depending on their function and location, they are not typically considered the longest cells in the human body. Many gland cells are relatively small and spherical, ranging from just a few micrometres to a few millimetres in diameter.

questions.explanation: The longest cell in the human body is the neuron or nerve cell. Some neurons can be up to three or four feet long, stretching from the base of the spine down to the toes. These long neurons are called the "longest axons" and are found in the human body's peripheral nervous system.

questions.text: What is the definition of "fitness" in terms of evolution?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: The organism's ability to attain resources while in competition with other organisms of its species
options.explanation: The organism's ability to attain resources while in competition with other organisms of its species is not a good measure of fitness because it does not take into account the number of offspring produced. An organism that is able to attain resources but does not produce any offspring will not be as fit as an organism that does not attain as many resources but produces more offspring.

options.text: The organism's ability to attract the most mates
options.explanation: The organism's ability to attract the most mates is not a good measure of fitness because it does not take into account whether the organism's offspring are able to survive and reproduce. An organism that is able to attract many mates but whose offspring do not survive will not be as fit as an organism that is able to attract fewer mates but whose offspring do survive.

The organism's health is not a good measure of fitness because it does not take into account the ability of the organism to reproduce. An organism that is healthy but does not produce any offspring will not be as fit as an organism that is not as healthy but produces more offspring.

options.text: The ability of an organism to contribute its genes to future generations
options.explanation: In terms of evolution, fitness is a measure of how well an organism is able to survive and reproduce in its environment. The more offspring an organism produces, the more likely its genes are to be passed on to the next generation. This means that the ability of an organism to contribute its genes to future generations is the best measure of its fitness.

Eventually, the host cell bursts open, or lyses, releasing the newly produced phages to infect other cells. options.explanation: Archaebacteria and Vibrio bacteria are two distinct types of microorganisms belonging to different domains and having different characteristics: Archaebacteria (Archaea): Archaebacteria, or simply Archaea, are a group of single-celled microorganisms that were initially classified as bacteria due to their small size and simple structure. However, research revealed that they are fundamentally different from true bacteria, leading to their reclassification into a separate domain called Archaea. Archaea are one of the three main domains of life, alongside Bacteria and Eukarya. Vibrio Bacteria: Vibrio bacteria are a group of Gram-negative, facultative anaerobic bacteria. They are typically comma-shaped or curved rods and are part of the Proteobacteria phylum.

Streptococcus: Streptococcus is a specific genus of bacteria within the phylum Firmicutes. These bacteria are characterized by their spherical or ovoid shape and their tendency to form chains of cells, resembling a string of beads when viewed under a microscope.

They were once classified as a type of bacteria, but later studies showed that they are more closely related to eukaryotes. Archaea can live in extreme environments, such as hot springs, hydrothermal vents, and salty lakes.

Cyanobacteria (Blue-Green Algae): Cyanobacteria are photosynthetic bacteria that possess pigments allowing them to perform photosynthesis. They are responsible for oxygen production in the early Earth and are capable of forming colonies and filaments.

questions.text: During transport of carbon dioxide, blood does not become acidic due to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: During the transport of carbon dioxide, the blood does not become acidic due to the presence of buffering systems in the blood.When carbon dioxide (CO2) is produced by the body's cells, it diffuses into the blood and combines with water (H2O) to form carbonic acid (H2CO3). Carbonic acid then dissociates into bicarbonate ions (HCO3-) and hydrogen ions (H+), which can contribute to the acidity of the blood if not properly regulated. However, the body has several buffering systems that can help maintain the pH of the blood within a narrow range, even in the presence of excess CO2. One of the most important buffering systems is the bicarbonate buffer system, which involves the reversible reaction between carbonic acid and bicarbonate ions.

Sodium carbonate (Na2CO3) can indeed neutralize carbonic acid (H2CO3) as a base, but this scenario is not the primary mechanism that prevents blood from becoming acidic during carbon dioxide transport. The buffering system mentioned above, involving bicarbonate ions, is primarily responsible for maintaining blood pH stability. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

Leukocytes, also known as white blood cells, play a vital role in the immune system and are involved in combating infections and foreign substances. While they can absorb and remove carbon dioxide, their role is not specifically related to preventing blood from becoming acidic. The buffering system is the primary mechanism responsible for pH regulation. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Non-accumulation is not a specific mechanism that prevents blood from becoming acidic during carbon dioxide transport. It is a general term that does not provide a specific explanation for pH regulation. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

questions.text: Which fluid bathes the neurons of brain and spinal cord and cushions against the bumps and jolts?

options.explanation: Blood is a specialized bodily fluid that circulates throughout the body via the circulatory system, delivering oxygen and nutrients.

Interstitial fluid is a clear, colorless fluid that surrounds and bathes the cells of tissues in the body. It is derived from the blood plasma that leaks out of the capillaries and into the interstitial space, which is the space between the cells and the blood vessels.

Intracellular fluid (ICF) is the fluid that is present inside the cells of the body. It is one of the two main compartments of body fluid, the other being extracellular fluid (ECF), which includes interstitial fluid, blood plasma, and other fluids outside of the cells.

Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) is a clear, colorless liquid that surrounds the brain and spinal cord. CSF serves several important functions in the central nervous system. It helps to cushion and protect the brain and spinal cord from injury, providing buoyant support for the delicate structures of the brain.

questions.text: Tobacco plants, resistant to a nematode, have been developed by the introduction of DNA that produces (in the host cells):
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Sense and antisense RNA are involved in gene regulation, but in the context of nematode resistance in plants, the introduction of DNA that produces a toxic protein is a more common approach.

This is the correct answer. Many genetically modified plants, including some tobacco plants, have been developed with the introduction of DNA that produces toxic proteins as a defense against pests, including nematodes. These proteins can be toxic to the pests, providing resistance to the plant.

questions.explanation: Many nematodes live in plants and animals including human beings. a nematode Meloidogyne incognita infects tobacco plants and causes a great reduction in yield. A novel strategy was adopted to prevent this infection that was based on the process of RNA interference. RNA interference is the phenomenon of inhibiting the activity of a gene by the synthesis of RNA molecules complementary to the mRNA. The normal messenger RNA of a gene is said to be ‘sense’ because it carries the codon that is ‘read’ during translation. Normally the complement to the mRNA ‘sense’ strand will not contain a sequence of codons that can be translated to produce a functional protein thus this complementary stand is called antisense RNA. The antisense RNA and messenger RNA molecules will anneal to form duplex RNA molecules and the duplex RNA molecules cannot be translated. Thus the presence of antisense RNA will block the translation of the Messenger RNA of the affected gene. Recent evidence indicates that these RNA duplexes are often rapidly degraded in Vivo.

questions.text: Golden rice is a promising transgenic crop. When released for cultivation, it will help in:

Golden rice is a genetically modified variety of rice that has been engineered to produce beta-carotene, a precursor of vitamin A. Vitamin A deficiency is a significant health issue in many parts of the world, particularly in developing countries where rice is a dietary staple. The incorporation of beta-carotene in golden rice can help address this deficiency, as it allows people to obtain a dietary source of vitamin A from rice consumption. This has the potential to improve the health and well-being of populations that rely heavily on rice as a primary food source.

Pest-resistant transgenic crops are genetically modified to be less susceptible to pests. They can produce toxins harmful to pests or bolster their defenses, reducing the need for chemical pesticides and protecting crops from pest damage. This can increase crop yields and reduce the environmental impact of agriculture.

Transgenic crops with herbicide tolerance are genetically modified to withstand specific herbicides. This allows farmers to use these herbicides to control weeds without harming the crop, simplifying weed management and reducing the need for manual labor.

questions.text: The principle of a direct current generator is based on:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The principle of a direct current (DC) generator is based on the concept of electromagnetic induction, which was discovered by Michael Faraday in the early 19th century. An electric generator is a device that converts mechanical energy into electrical energy by exploiting the principles of electromagnetic induction.In a DC generator, a magnetic field is produced by a set of stationary magnets, while a conductor is rotated within the magnetic field to generate an electrical current. As the conductor rotates, it cuts across the magnetic lines of force, which induces an electromotive force (EMF) in the conductor. This EMF produces a current flow in the circuit connected to the generator, which can be used to power electrical devices.

questions.text: The object oscillates due to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: An object oscillates due to the presence of a restoring force that tries to bring the object back to its equilibrium position when it is displaced from it. When an object is displaced from its equilibrium position and a restoring force acts on it, the object undergoes oscillatory motion, which means it moves back and forth around its equilibrium position

The weight of an object is the force exerted on it due to gravity. While the weight may affect the motion of an object, it does not directly cause oscillatory motion. In some cases, the weight may act as a restoring force, such as in the case of a pendulum where the gravitational force provides the restoring force that brings the pendulum back to its equilibrium position. However, in general, the weight alone does not cause oscillations.

Centripetal force is the force that acts on an object moving in a circular path, directing it toward the center of the circle. While centripetal force is essential for maintaining circular motion, it does not cause oscillatory motion. Oscillations typically involve back-and-forth motion along a straight or curved path, rather than continuous circular motion.

Frictional force opposes the motion of an object and acts parallel to the surface with which the object is in contact. While friction may affect the motion of an object, it does not generally cause oscillatory motion. Friction tends to dissipate energy and dampen motion rather than sustain periodic motion characteristic of oscillations.

options.explanation: This option suggests a pressure that is significantly higher than the initial pressure of the gas. However, when the volume increases, and the temperature rises, according to the ideal gas law, the pressure is expected to decrease. Therefore, this option does not accurately represent the expected pressure of the gas after the change in volume and temperature.

To solve this problem, we can use the combined gas law:(P1V1)/T1 = (P2V2)/T2where P1, V1, and T1 are the initial pressure, volume, and temperature, respectively, and P2, V2, and T2 are the final pressure, volume, and temperature, respectively.Substituting the given values, we get:(1.72 × 10^5 Pa)(2.81 m^3)/(15.5 + 273.15 K) = (P2)(4.16 m^3)/(28.2 + 273.15 K)Solving for P2, we get:P2 = [(1.72 × 10^5 Pa)(2.81 m^3)/(15.5 + 273.15 K)] × [(28.2 + 273.15 K)/(4.16 m^3)]= 1.06 × 10^5 PaTherefore, the pressure of the gas is approximately 1.06 × 10^5 Pa when the volume is raised to 4.16 m^3 and the temperature is raised to 28.2 °C.

options.explanation: The work done by the cyclist on the road can be calculated using the formula: Work = Force × Distance × cos(θ), where θ is the angle between the force and the direction of motion (180 degrees in this case, since the force is opposite to the motion). questions.text: The ionization power of _ ray is highest.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Among the three types of common radiation, alpha particles have the highest ionization power.Alpha particles are composed of two protons and two neutrons, and they have a positive charge. When they pass through matter, they interact strongly with the electrons and atoms of the material, causing a large number of ionizations. This means that they can knock off electrons from atoms, creating positively charged ions and free electrons, which can go on to create further ionizations.In comparison, beta and gamma radiation have lower ionization power.

questions.text: Statement: Should children be prevented completely from watching television? Arguments: (I) No. We get vital information regarding education through television. (II) Yes. It hampers the study of children. (Ill) Yes. Young children are misguided by certain programmes featuring violence.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Only I, II and Ill are strong
options.explanation: Both arguments (I) and (III) are strong. Argument (I) states that children should not be completely prevented from watching television because television provides vital information regarding education. This argument highlights the educational value of television and how it can be a valuable source of learning for children. Restricting television entirely could deprive them of educational content that can supplement their studies and knowledge. Argument (III) states that children should be prevented from watching television because certain programs featuring violence can misguide young children. This argument points out a potential negative impact of television on young minds, especially when they are exposed to violent or inappropriate content. Shielding children from such content can protect their mental and emotional well-being. Both arguments present valid points, and a balanced approach would be necessary in addressing the role of television in a child's life. It is essential to provide access to educational content while also ensuring that exposure to potentially harmful or unsuitable material is minimized or restricted. Parental guidance and responsible media choices can help strike the right balance in children's television viewing habits. Since argument (II) is not strong, this option is incorrect.

Clearly, television offers various educational programs which are of great practical value to students. So, it serves as a means (but it is not the 'only' means) to educate the masses. Thus, I hold strong while IV does not. Besides, the demerits of watching television, mentioned in II and III, may be done away with by allowing children to watch selected programs on television, according to a set schedule. So, neither II nor III holds strong.

questions.text: Verbal Classification If a - b = 3 and a2 + b2 = 29, find the value of ab.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: Solve using substitution A-B = 3. A = 3+B. WE KNOW THAT, A^2+B^2 = 29. (3+B) ^2+B^2 = 29. 9+B^2+6B+B^2 = 29. 2B^2+6B-20 = 0. B^2+3B-10 = 0. B = 2 & B = -5. THEN TAKE +VALVE B = 2. WE KNOW THAT A-B = 3. Hence A-2 = 3. A = 5. AB = 2*5 = 10.
